metadata.title: States PCS: Karnataka GK for KPSC and other Karnataka State Exams

questions.question: What is Karnataka’s targeted total revenue receipt for 2026-27?
questions.explanation: Karnataka’s state budget for 2026-27 targets total revenue receipts of ₹2.2 lakh crore. The budget was presented by CM Siddaramaiah on March 27, 2026. The target represents a 13.5% increase from previous figures. GST comprises about 43% of Karnataka’s own tax revenue. The budget projects a revenue deficit of ₹22,957 crore due to GST rationalisation losses of approximately ₹15,000 crore. Central tax devolution to the state is estimated at ₹63,049 crore, representing 4.13% of total receipts.

questions.question: What was Karnataka’s renewable energy capacity on October 31, 2025?
questions.explanation: Karnataka’s renewable energy capacity reached 25,675.39 MW (25.7 GW) by October 31, 2025. This accounted for approximately 10.24% of India’s total renewable capacity of 250,643.45 MW. In the first quarter of 2025, Karnataka added 1,316 MW, primarily from renewable sources. Nationally, India’s total installed power capacity reached 520.6 GW by January 2026, including 272 GW of non-fossil fuel capacity. Data after October 2025 lacks state-wise detail from the Central Electricity Authority.

questions.question: What is Karnataka’s rank in total installed power generation capacity as of February 2026?
questions.explanation: Karnataka ranks 6th among Indian states in total installed power generation capacity as of February 2026. Its total capacity is about 23 GW, including 7.1 GW of large-scale solar power, the highest in India. Karnataka’s renewable capacity totals 25.7 GW as of October 2025. Other states like Rajasthan, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, and Maharashtra have higher capacities. National installed capacity reached 513.73 GW by that time. Karnataka’s peak power demand was 94 GW in FY 2025-26, ranking 6th in supply position. Data sources include NITI Aayog and PIB reports.

questions.question: Yusuf Arakkal of Karnataka is known for which painting style?
questions.explanation: Yusuf Arakkal was a prominent contemporary artist from Bengaluru, Karnataka. He developed a figurative and expressionist painting style. His artwork often portrayed themes of urban loneliness, human suffering, and existential concerns. Arakkal used bold outlines and muted colors to create impactful visual stories. His contributions shaped Karnataka’s modern art scene and earned national and international recognition.

questions.question: Regarding the literary contributions of Kuavempu (K.V. Puttappa), which of the following works were authored by him? Sri Ramayana Darshanam Jaya Bharata Jananiya Tanujate Kavirajamarga Malegalalli Madumagalu Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Kuvempu, a Jnanpith awardee, authored Sri Ramayana Darshanam (his magnum opus), Jaya Bharata Jananiya Tanujate (Karnataka’s state anthem), and Malegalalli Madumagalu (a famous novel). Kavirajamarga is not his work; it is a classical text on Kannada poetics attributed to Rashtrakuta King Amoghavarsha I.

questions.question: Who wrote the satirical column ‘Choobaana’ in Prajavani newspaper?
questions.explanation: T.S. Ramachandra Rao was the editor of Kannada daily Prajavani. He wrote the satirical column ‘Choobaana’ known for sharp wit and political satire. Rao also authored a Shivaji Maharaj biography and encouraged Kannada literary talents.

questions.question: Which king patronized the Kannada poet “Adi Kavi” Pampa?
questions.explanation: Arikesari II was a ruler of the Vemulavada Chalukya dynasty and patron of Pampa. He was a feudatory of Rashtrakuta Emperor Krishna III. Arikesari II honored Pampa with the title Kavita Gunarnava for his epic “Vikramarjuna Vijaya” or “Pampa Bharata.” Pampa identified Arikesari II with Arjuna in his work. For his contributions, Pampa received the village Dharmapura (now in Telangana) as an Agrahara from Arikesari II.

questions.question: What is the real name of the poet Kumaravyasa?
questions.explanation: Kumaravyasa’s real name was Naranappa. He is also known as Gadugina Naranappa because he came from Koliwada village near Gadag in North Karnataka. He wrote the Karnata Bharata Kathamanjari and took the pen name “Kumara Vyasa” in reverence to Sage Vyasa, the original author of the Mahabharata.

questions.question: “Hana” (ಹಾನ) is a dialect word of which Kannada regional dialect?
questions.explanation: The Dharwad Kannada dialect, spoken in North Karnataka, often drops or adds the aspirated ‘H’ sound. In this dialect, the standard Kannada word “Hana” (money) is pronounced by dropping the ‘H’ as “Ana.” This phonetic change is common in regions like Hubballi-Dharwad and Belagavi. Other dialects like Mysore, Mangalore, and Gulbarga Kannada do not exhibit this particular linguistic tendency.

questions.question: Which Karnataka spot is declared a National Geological Monument?
questions.explanation: St. Mary’s Island, off Malpe coast in Udupi district, was declared a National Geological Monument by the Geological Survey of India in 1975. It is known for unique hexagonal basalt columns formed about 88 million years ago due to volcanic activity. The island comprises four smaller islands including Coconut Island, where these rock formations are most visible.
